metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of peripheral arterial occlusive disease of the lower extremities

rules.rule_text: The criteria used for diagnosis of LEAD in patients recruited for clinical trials must be clearly defined. The diagnosis, type of occlusive lesion (stenosis, complete block) and its location must be confirmed by objective means.
rules.verbatim_quote: The criteria used for the diagnosis of LEAD in patients recruited for clinical trials must be clearly defined. The diagnosis, type of occlusive lesion (stenosis, complete block) and its location must be confirmed by objective means.

rules.rule_text: A disease stage-specific approach must be adopted for the choice of study population and clinical endpoints in confirmatory therapeutic studies, making efficacy results fully evaluable.
rules.verbatim_quote: This latter point is an important requirement in confirmatory therapeutic studies, for which a disease stage-specific approach can be adopted in the choice of the study population and clinical endpoints, thus making efficacy results fully evaluable.

rules.rule_text: The study population must be homogenous in terms of disease severity, localization, and risk of disease progression. Both clinical and anatomical classification systems must be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: The choice of the different classification systems and their combination is expected to be adequate to the definition of a study population that should be homogenous in terms of disease severity, localization, and risk of disease progression. While a symptomatology-based grading supports the definition of stage of disease, anatomical classification systems also incorporating, but not limited to the angiosome concept, are essential elements to be considered.

rules.rule_text: For CLTI patients, the definition of 'no or poor revascularisation options' must be tightly defined in the study protocol. Treatment groups must be balanced for patient demography, severity of disease, previous revascularisation procedures, concomitant medications, and rehabilitation programs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since revascularisation should be offered to all CLTI patients, the definition of "no or poor revascularisation options" needs to be tightly defined in the study protocol. Treatment groups should be balanced in respect of patient demography, severity of disease, previous revascularisation procedures and duration of symptoms, as well as concomitant medications and standardized rehabilitation programs, as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: A distinction between type 1 and type 2 diabetes must be considered in study design, based on reported differences in clinical outcomes and therapeutic management.
rules.verbatim_quote: A distinction between type 1 and type 2 diabetes should also be considered, based on the reported differences in terms of clinical outcomes and therapeutic management between the two populations.

rules.rule_text: Claudication distances must be assessed using a standardised, reproducible test methodology (treadmill test or 6MWD test). Two internationally accepted treadmill protocols (constant workload and graded) are acceptable but cannot be used interchangeably. The treadmill protocol must be decided beforehand and not altered.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Treatment endpoints - Improvement of walking capacity
rules.verbatim_quote: Claudication distances should be assessed using a standardised, reproducible test methodology (i.e. treadmill test or 6MWD test). There are two internationally accepted treadmill protocols... Both tests can be equally recommended for use in clinical trials but cannot be used in an interchangeable way; a decision on the treadmill protocol and the treadmill settings must be made beforehand and should not be altered.

rules.rule_text: The 6MWD can be used as a primary endpoint as it has been demonstrated to be representative of daily life walking functionality and correlates with daily physical activity and mortality outcomes.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Treatment endpoints - Improvement of walking capacity
rules.verbatim_quote: The 6MWD can be used as a primary endpoint, considering it has been demonstrated to be representative enough of daily life walking functionality, and is even more correlated with daily physical activity than the historically more often used treadmill testing (McDermott 2014). The 6MWD was found also to correlate with mortality outcomes (McDermott 2011).

rules.rule_text: The minimal clinically important difference (MCID) for walking distance endpoints must be pre-specified in the study protocol and justified for the specific targeted population.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Treatment endpoints - Improvement of walking capacity
rules.verbatim_quote: The minimal clinically important difference (MCID) that is intended to be used in the inference of efficacy of treatment requires to be pre-specified in the study protocol and is expected to be justified and relevant to the specific targeted population.

rules.rule_text: If ICD is chosen as primary endpoint, ACD must be evaluated as secondary. If ACD is chosen as primary, ICD must be evaluated as secondary. If a graded treadmill protocol is used, ACD must be the primary efficacy variable.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Treatment endpoints - Improvement of walking capacity
rules.verbatim_quote: If ICD is chosen as primary endpoint, ACD should be evaluated as a secondary endpoint. ... if a graded treadmill protocol is used, ACD should be the primary efficacy variable. ... If ACD is chosen as primary endpoint, ICD should be evaluated as a secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Pain intensity must be quantified using standardised methods (e.g. visual analogue scale). A responder analysis requires pre-specification of clinically important change thresholds. Pain assessment methodology must be standardised including time of assessment, personnel, and analgesic consumption.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pain (if existing) should be quantified. The intensity of the pain should be assessed by means of standardised methods (e. g. visual analogue scale). A responder analysis requires a pre-specification of changes in pain intensity ratings that will be of clinical importance. ... the standardisation of the trial methodology is of utmost importance. This does not only refer to the methodology used to quantify pain but does include factors such as the time of pain assessment (same time of the day, preferably at drug trough levels), the personnel taking the measurement (which should not change), and the assessment of analgesic consumption.

rules.rule_text: Ulcer healing must be defined as healing of all ischaemic ulcers in both legs (all ulcers epithelialized), assessed by an independent physician and documented by photography. Only total healing of lesions should be reported as main efficacy criterion.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ulcer healing must be defined as healing of all ischaemic ulcers in both legs (all ulcers epithelialized as assessed by an independent physician and documented by photography). Since quantification of partial healing may be difficult to assess objectively and since the clinical relevance of partial healing remains unclear, only total healing of lesions should be reported as main efficacy criterion.

rules.rule_text: Only major amputations (above the ankle) should be counted for the assessment of amputation rates. Both legs must be considered. Criteria for major amputations must be specified a priori in the study protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Only major amputations, above the ankle, should be counted (unlike minor amputations, i.e below the ankle). Both legs must be considered for the assessment of amputation rates. The criteria for major amputations are to be specified a priori in the study protocol to avoid relevant centre-related effects.

rules.rule_text: Cardiovascular morbidity alone must not serve as a single primary endpoint but may be incorporated into a composite primary endpoint which also includes mortality.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cardiovascular morbidity alone should not serve as a single primary endpoint, but it could be incorporated into a composite primary endpoint which also includes mortality.

rules.rule_text: The most adequate endpoint in prevention studies is a composite endpoint specified a priori, consisting of clinically relevant components including cardiovascular morbidity (stroke, MI), and all-cause mortality. Major amputation should be included in the composite for stages III/IV and CLTI.
rules.verbatim_quote: The most adequate endpoint in prevention studies is a composite endpoint, if specified a priori, and if consisting of clinically relevant components. Such an endpoint may include cardiovascular morbidity (e.g. stroke, myocardial infarction) and all-cause mortality. Major amputation as a component of a composite endpoint can also be considered and should be generally adopted in more severe stages (stages III/IV, CLTI).

rules.rule_text: A significant effect in a composite endpoint must be coupled with evidence that none of the components is negatively influenced.
rules.verbatim_quote: efficacy in the composite endpoint should be coupled with evidence that none of the components is negatively influenced.

rules.rule_text: Studies must have a confirmatory statistical approach with demonstration of superiority, equivalence, or non-inferiority pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies aiming at the proof of efficacy must have a confirmatory statistical approach - e.g. a demonstration of superiority, equivalence or non-inferiority must be pre-specified in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Pivotal trials must robustly demonstrate statistically significant effects of a relevant clinical magnitude.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the pivotal trials robustly demonstrate statistically significant effects of a relevant clinical magnitude.

rules.rule_text: The estimand of interest must be defined with a disease-specific approach, driven by the intended use and target population. The study protocol must identify relevant intercurrent events and clearly define strategies to handle them.
rules.verbatim_quote: A disease-specific approach should be adopted for the definition of the primary and (key) secondary estimands, with the definition of the main clinical endpoints to be driven by the intended use of an investigational drug and target population... It is expected that the study protocol identifies relevant intercurrent events and clearly defines strategies to handle them.

rules.rule_text: For symptomatic treatment studies, a randomised parallel group, double-blind, placebo-controlled design is generally required. Active drug-controlled trials without placebo may only be considered if the comparator has consistently shown superiority over placebo.
rules.verbatim_quote: A randomised parallel group, double-blind, placebo-controlled design is generally required. Placebo should be used for the control group since suitable reference substances have not yet been established in the symptomatic treatment of intermittent claudication, as well as in more advanced stages of disease. Active drug-controlled trials without a placebo arm may only be considered if the comparator drug has consistently shown superiority over placebo (assay sensitivity, see ICH E10).

rules.rule_text: A run-in phase of 2-6 weeks is recommended to verify the stability of the patient's conditions (comedication, stability of claudication distances).
rules.verbatim_quote: A run-in phase of 2-6 weeks is recommended to verify the stability of the patient's conditions, e.g. comedication, stability of the claudication distances.

rules.rule_text: The active treatment phase must normally last a minimum of 6 months for an investigational medicinal product intended for chronic therapy. The cumulative duration of active treatment and follow-up must not be less than 6 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: The active treatment phase should normally last for a minimum of 6 months for administration of an investigational medicinal product intended for chronic therapy. ... the cumulative duration of active treatment phase and follow-up period should not be less than 6 months for a controlled trial.
rules.exceptions: Shorter lengths are expected to be adequately justified depending on nature of product, mode of action, intended indication.

rules.rule_text: For claudicant patients, a history of typical intermittent claudication lasting at least 6 months is expected. The clinical diagnosis must be confirmed by objective evidence (e.g. reduced ankle systolic blood pressure).
rules.verbatim_quote: For claudicant patients, a history of typical intermittent claudication lasting for at least 6 months to ensure clinical stability is expected. The clinical diagnosis of LEAD should be confirmed by objective evidence (e. g. reduced ankle systolic blood pressure).

rules.rule_text: Patients with high variability in walking distance must be excluded. At least two treadmill tests with a time interval >= 1 week must be performed, with maximum change in ACD not exceeding a predefined threshold (e.g. 25%).
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that patients with high variability in the walking distance be excluded. For this purpose, at least two treadmill tests should be performed with a timeinterval of >= 1 week. The maximum change in the claudication distance should not exceed a predefined threshold [e.g. 25 % for the absolute claudication distance (ACD)].

rules.rule_text: Walking training is the first treatment option for claudicant patients. All patients must have tried this therapeutic measure before being considered for entering the trial, unless otherwise justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Walking training is considered the first treatment option in this patient population. Therefore, it is expected that this therapeutic measure is tried for all patients before they are considered for entering the trial, unless otherwise justified.

rules.rule_text: Only patients with rest pain due to chronic critical limb ischaemia (persistent recurrent pain at rest requiring analgesics for more than 2 weeks) should be considered for rest pain studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Only patients with rest pain due to chronic critical limb ischaemia, that is defined as persistent recurrent pain at rest requiring analgesics for more than 2 weeks should be considered.

rules.rule_text: For CLTI patients, the diagnosis currently requires objectively documented atherosclerosis based on combined evaluation of pressure measurements and Doppler arterial waveforms corroborated by vascular imaging, combined with rest pain for at least 2 weeks.
rules.verbatim_quote: As per all patients with CLTI, the diagnosis currently requires objectively documented atherosclerosis, based on a combined evaluation of pressure measurements and Doppler arterial waveforms corroborated by the vascular imaging, combined with rest pain for at least 2 weeks.

rules.rule_text: It is strongly recommended to study diabetic and non-diabetic patients in separate trials or to use appropriate stratification.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is strongly recommended to study diabetic patients and non-diabetic patients in separate trials or to use appropriate stratification.

rules.rule_text: For claudication studies, walking distance must be the primary symptomatic endpoint. For rest pain patients, relief of pain at rest is the main symptomatic efficacy endpoint. For skin lesions, complete healing of all necrosis and ulcerations is the main symptomatic efficacy endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: For claudication studies, walking distance should be the primary symptomatic endpoint. For patients with rest pain, the main symptomatic efficacy endpoint is the relief of pain at rest. ... In the presence of skin lesions, the main symptomatic efficacy endpoint is complete healing of all necrosis and ulcerations.

rules.rule_text: For rest pain, it must be shown that the investigational product has no analgesic properties and pain reduction is the downstream effect of its pharmacological action.
rules.verbatim_quote: It must be shown that the investigational medicinal product has no analgesic properties in terms of mode of action, although pain reduction is the downstream effect of its pharmacological action.

rules.rule_text: For prevention of disease progression trials, a randomised, parallel group, double-blind, controlled study design is generally required. Treatment should last a minimum of 12 months, with longer periods recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: A randomised, parallel group, double-blind, controlled study design is generally required. Placebo and/or active drug-controlled trials may be adequate. Treatment should last for a minimum of 12 months, but longer periods are recommended.

rules.rule_text: Patients with intermittent claudication and CLTI must be studied separately, or appropriate stratification techniques must be applied. Diabetics and non-diabetics must be studied separately or stratified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with intermittent claudication and CLTI should be studied separately, or appropriate stratification techniques should be applied... Diabetics and non-diabetics should be studied separately or, if included in the same study, this distinction should serve as stratification factor.

rules.rule_text: A new agent in LEAD is only acceptable for registration if there is no negative impact on mortality and cardiovascular morbidity. A separate analysis on all-cause mortality, cardiovascular morbidity, and vascular death must be made from pivotal trials if not already the efficacy endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: If not investigated as efficacy endpoint, a separate analysis on all-cause mortality, cardiovascular morbidity / and vascular death should be made on basis of the pivotal clinical trials. A new agent in LEAD is only acceptable for registration if there is no negative impact on mortality and cardiovascular morbidity.

rules.rule_text: Effects on cardiac conduction (PR, QRS, QT and QTc) must be documented. Neurohumoral compensatory mechanisms, heart rate, ECG and Holter monitoring must be performed at frequent intervals throughout the study.
rules.source_section: 8.2 Neurohumoral activation and pro-arrhythmic and/or pro-anginal effects
rules.verbatim_quote: Depending on the particular pharmacodynamic properties of the new agent, measurement of effects on neurohumoral compensatory mechanisms, heart rate, ECG and Holter monitoring should be performed at frequent intervals throughout the study. Effects on cardiac conduction (PR, QRS, QT and QTc) should be documented.

rules.rule_text: For ATMPs in CLTI, wound healing, pain control and limb salvage must be the primary goal. The follow-up period must not be less than 6 months in controlled trials. Double-blindness must be maintained during the whole period.
rules.verbatim_quote: the follow-up period should not be less than 6 months in controlled trials. ... Double-blindness should be maintained during the whole period. ... Wound healing, pain control and limb salvage should be the primary goal for ATMPs in patients with CLTI who have no options for revascularization.

rules.rule_text: For ATMPs, adverse events of special interest must be pre-specified in the study design, including allergic/immunologic reactions, severe infections, CRP increases, MACE, rest pain increase and/or ulcer worsening, and incidence of tumours. An appropriate risk management plan for post-marketing monitoring is always required.
rules.verbatim_quote: adverse events of special interest should be identified and pre-specified in the study design, including but not limited to allergic/immunologic reactions, severe infections, adverse events of CRP increases, specific AEs of MACE, MI/UA, strokes, AEs of rest pain increase and/or ulcer worsening, incidence of tumours. An appropriate risk management plan is always required to monitor events in the post-marketing phase.
